title: pimelea villosa subsp. arenaria
intro: Pimelea villosa subsp. arenaria, also known as the hairy rice flower or sandhill rice flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pimelea villosa subsp. arenaria include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of Pimelea villosa subsp. arenaria can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
